title: Black Rock Senegal Artist Residency
description: Black Rock Senegal, founded by artist Kehinde Wiley, hosts visual artists, writers, and filmmakers for one-to-three-month residencies on the coast of Dakar. Residents receive private studio space, room and board, and a stipend for supplies and cultural activities, selected through an annual open call with no application fee.
visaNote: Most nationalities can enter Senegal visa-free for tourist/short stays under 90 days, covering the typical 1-3 month residency; check current requirements for your passport.

title: African Development Bank Internship Programme
description: The AfDB's Internship Programme places master's-level students and recent graduates in 3-6 month internships at the Bank's headquarters in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire, or regional offices, working across departments such as economics, finance, and development operations. Interns receive a monthly stipend and medical insurance for the duration of the placement.
visaNote: Open only to nationals of AfDB member countries (54 African states plus 28 non-regional shareholder countries); non-Ivorian interns must arrange visa/entry for on-site placement in Abidjan.

title: African Union Digital & Innovation Fellowship
description: The AU Digital & Innovation Fellowship places young African tech professionals in 12-month placements inside African Union Commission departments in Addis Ababa, working on digital transformation, public-sector innovation, and governance projects. Fellows relocate to Addis Ababa and receive a monthly stipend of EUR 2,500; the program is hosted by the African Union and implemented by enpact with funding from Germany's BMZ/GIZ DataCipation programme.
visaNote: Fellows relocate to Addis Ababa for 12 months; open only to citizens of African Union member states, who typically need a visa/residence permit for Ethiopia, arranged with programme support.

title: UN Volunteer Assignments in Tanzania
description: UN Volunteers (UNV) recruits international volunteers for assignments with UN agencies in Tanzania, including UNHCR's refugee response operations and UNDP development programming. Assignments are posted individually through UNV's Global Talent Pool rather than a single fixed annual intake.
visaNote: UNV and the host UN agency coordinate visa/entry authorization; a Volunteer Living Allowance covers housing, food and transport but the exact amount varies by duty station and is not published as a fixed figure.

title: Rotary Peace Fellowship - Professional Development Certificate (Makerere University)
description: Rotary's Peace Center at Makerere University in Kampala, Uganda offers a fully-funded, one-year blended-learning Professional Development Certificate in peace and development for working professionals, including a two-week online course, a 10-week on-site course with field studies in Kampala, and a nine-month remote implementation project. The fellowship covers tuition, room and board, round-trip transportation, and field-study expenses; up to 40 fellowships are awarded per cohort.
org: Rotary International / Rotary Peace Center, Makerere University
visaNote: Open to applicants of any nationality who demonstrate a compelling interest in African peacebuilding; Uganda entry-visa requirements vary by passport and are the fellow's responsibility to check.

title: Princeton in Latin America Fellowship - Guatemala
description: Princeton in Latin America (PiLA), an independent nonprofit unaffiliated with government exchange programs, places recent college graduates in year-long fellowships with partner NGOs in Guatemala, one of its longest-running partner countries. Fellows receive a monthly stipend covering housing, food and local transport (amount varies by placement), plus insurance, though airfare is the fellow's own cost.
visaNote: Open to graduates of accredited U.S. and Canadian universities within about five years of their bachelor's degree; fellows arrange their own visa status for Guatemala and cover round-trip airfare and field insurance themselves.
